message: Jake
Yuriko
Office
− The Next Day −
What a disaster I went through yesterday.
With a gloomy head since morning, I still headed
to work.
With Meryl's request pretty much done, it would’ve been fine to go on vacation for a while.
I was thinking about that.
When I arrived and put my hand on the doorknob, I noticed the door, which should've been locked, was open.
I cautiously opened the door,
and stepped inside.
W-What the hell is this?
The place was a complete mess. Someone must have snuck in here and ransacked it last night or maybe this morning.
But who? What on earth could be their motive for this?
For now, I checked to make sure no one was in the room and began to search around.
Search
System
Desk
Window
Floor
Documents
Wall
Save
Exit the game
I searched everything, but I can’t tell what was taken or if these cufflinks are the culprits'.
Everything
won't be clear until they come.
Good morning.
Gah! Boss, what’s going on here!?
Oh, good morning, I’ve been waiting for you. We're doing a big cleaning starting today.
W-What?
What!? No way!
I can't find anything else.
Oh, something's fallen under the desk.
These cufflinks have a rare design. Their vibe somewhat reminds me of Gare's jewelry.
But if this isn't mine,
then it must belong to the intruder.
My desk has been flipped 180 degrees, and several drawers are sticking out.
However, since the phone and computer on the desk are untouched, it doesn't seem like a typical burglar.
Luckily, I didn't have anything valuable lying around, but what was the intruder's goal?
The blinds are bent, but the window glass isn't broken. I guess that's a small blessing.
Seeing that the window is intact, the intruder must have entered through the door.
But I really can't figure out the motive of this non-violent intruder.
Looks like I'll need to replace the blinds.
The floor is littered with papers and trash that have spilled from the bin. Cleaning this up is going to be a pain.
Ugh.
Hmm, I wonder if anything noteworthy is lying around.
I hope to find something that might give me a clue about the intruder.
I'll clean up after this.
Ah.
Ah, what a hassle since this morning.
Good thing there weren't many important documents. Most of it is bills and stuff.
and lists of detective novels and crime data printed out.
The intruder didn't come here to throw out trash, nor did they just vandalize out of frustration.
Everything's probably just the kind of paperwork I could toss into the trash.
Ah.
Ah, my favorite poster has been torn down. What on earth was this intruder aiming for?
It really doesn’t look like a simple theft.
Looking at it this way, what a dirty wall it is.
Sigh.
※※※ Bug detected ※※※
